<!-- EB5-SCAFFOLD v1 | generated by eb5-prepare | structural guidance only — contains NO investor financial figures or PII | safe-to-read -->

# Privacy & handling — read before you put anything in this package

This package is a **scaffold**: folders, checklists, and blank templates that help you
assemble a complete EB-5 petition package so your attorney has to ask for fewer things.
**The tool that built it never saw, asked for, or stored any of your sensitive data.** You
add the real documents and figures yourself, locally, after the scaffold is created.

## What counts as sensitive here (handle with care)

- **Money specifics** — account/routing numbers, balances, exact dollar amounts, wire
  confirmations, transaction lines, tax figures.
- **Identity / PII** — passport, national ID, SSN/ITIN, USCIS A-number, date of birth,
  home address, signatures.
- **Source-of-funds proof** — bank/brokerage statements, tax returns, sale deeds,
  business financials, gift/loan agreements, transfer receipts.

These live in the numbered folders (especially `01_Identity_and_Personal/` and
`02_Source_of_Funds/`). Treat everything in those folders as confidential.

## Rules of the road

1. **Keep sensitive values out of chat tools.** When you ask an assistant (including the
   `eb5-prepare` / `eb5-gapcheck` skills) to help, it works on **structure and
   completeness only**. Do not paste account numbers, balances, or scans of IDs into a
   chat. The skills are configured to refuse to store such values.
2. **Share through your attorney's secure channel**, not email attachments. Use their
   encrypted client portal / secure upload. If you must email, encrypt the file and send
   the password by a separate channel.
3. **Never commit this package to a shared/public repo.** A `.gitignore` is included that
   excludes the sensitive folders; do not override it. Better: keep the whole package off
   version control entirely.
4. **Redact before wide sharing.** When showing a document to anyone other than your own
   attorney, redact account numbers and PII you don't need to disclose.
5. **The independent project due-diligence** (`06_Project_Due_Diligence/`) is about the
   *project*, not about you — it contains no personal data and can be shared more freely.

## Why this exists

EB-5 source-of-funds review is the single biggest cause of back-and-forth and Requests
for Evidence (RFEs). A complete, well-organized, **privacy-safe** package speeds the
process and lowers the chance your data is mishandled along the way.

> This document is general organizational guidance, **not legal advice**. Your own
> immigration attorney decides what is required for your specific filing.
